answer:
\boxed{B}

Streptococci contribute to caries development primarily by producing acid as a byproduct of carbohydrate metabolism, which lowers the pH of the tooth surface and demineralizes enamel. While options C (extracellular polysaccharides) and D (fermentation) are related, acid production is the direct mechanism driving enamel dissolution. Resistance to penicillin (A) is irrelevant to caries formation.
